@@ -104,7 +104,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-80au","title":"Implement command palette (Cmd+K)","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-87ys. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Implement a command palette for quick navigation and actions. ## Features ### Trigger - Cmd+K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Windows) - Visible in header (optional button) - Focus immediately on open ### Search - Search prompts, collections, actions - Fuzzy matching - Recent items first - Category grouping ### Actions Available - Navigate to pages - Search prompts - Create new prompt - Open settings - Toggle dark mode - View keyboard shortcuts - Open documentation - Switch workspace (if applicable) ### Navigation Items - Home, Library, Search - Collections (list user's collections) - Settings, Billing, Profile - Admin (if admin user) ### Quick Actions - "New prompt" → Opens editor - "New collection" → Opens modal - "Copy last prompt" → Copies - "Export library" → Triggers export ### UI/UX - Modal overlay with backdrop - Search input at top - Grouped results below - Arrow key navigation - Enter to select - Escape to close - Recent searches shown ## Implementation - Use cmdk library (or similar) - Integrate with TanStack Query for data - Keyboard event handling - Focus trap in modal ## Files to Create - src/components/command-palette/index.tsx - src/components/command-palette/command-item.tsx - src/components/command-palette/command-group.tsx - src/hooks/use-command-palette.ts ## Success Criteria - Opens instantly - Search is fast - Actions work correctly - Keyboard navigation smooth - Accessible ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:26.469409772-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T21:35:22.166582528-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T21:35:22.166582528-05:00","close_reason":"Command palette already implemented as SpotlightSearch.tsx with: Cmd+K trigger, fuzzy search, category filtering, keyboard navigation (arrows/enter/escape), semantic search mode, recent searches, copy to clipboard, accessibility (role=dialog, aria labels, focus trap). See apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-80au","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3q8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:31.684540761-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-81d","title":"Implement jfp completion command for shell completions","description":"Part 6.1 of PLAN. 'jfp completion --shell zsh|bash|fish' outputs shell completion script. Completions for: command names, prompt IDs, category/tag values, bundle IDs. Usage: source <(jfp completion --shell zsh). Improves CLI discoverability and usability for power users.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:46:33.448582063-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:09:44.989090273-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:09:44.989090273-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented jfp completion command with bash/zsh/fish support in packages/cli/src/commands/completion.ts. Commit c7f6497.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-81d","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:49:26.819599989-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-82jx","title":"Implement user following and activity feed","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7ly5. ## Task Allow users to follow creators and see activity feed. ## Why This Matters - Social engagement - Stay updated on favorite creators - Build community - Increase retention ## Features ### 1. Following System - Follow/unfollow users - Following count display - Followers count display - Following list page ### 2. Activity Feed - New prompts from followed users - New collections from followed - Updates to followed content - Filtered by type ### 3. Notifications - New follower notification - Optional email digest - Activity summary ### 4. Discovery - Suggested users to follow - Popular creators - Similar to your interests ## Database Schema ```typescript export const userFollows = pgTable('user_follows', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), followerId: uuid('follower_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), followeeId: uuid('followee_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }, (table) => [ unique('uq_follow').on(table.followerId, table.followeeId), index('idx_follows_follower').on(table.followerId), index('idx_follows_followee').on(table.followeeId), ]); export const activityFeed = pgTable('activity_feed', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), // Feed owner actorId: uuid('actor_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), // Who did it activityType: text('activity_type').notNull(), // new_prompt, new_collection, etc. resourceType: text('resource_type'), resourceId: uuid('resource_id'), seen: boolean('seen').default(false), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }, (table) => [ index('idx_feed_user').on(table.userId), index('idx_feed_time').on(table.createdAt), ]); ``` ## Implementation ### Follow Actions ```typescript async function followUser(followerId: string, followeeId: string) { await db.insert(userFollows).values({ followerId, followeeId }); await createNotification(followeeId, 'new_follower', { followerId }); await updateFollowCounts(followerId, followeeId); } ``` ### Feed Generation ```typescript async function getFeed(userId: string, limit = 20) { // Get activities from followed users return db.select() .from(activityFeed) .where(eq(activityFeed.userId, userId)) .orderBy(desc(activityFeed.createdAt)) .limit(limit); } ``` ### Fan-out on Write When user creates content: 1. Get all followers 2. Insert feed entry for each follower 3. Batch insert for efficiency ## UI Components - FollowButton - Follow/unfollow - FollowStats - Follower/following counts - ActivityFeed - Scrollable feed - SuggestedUsers - Users to follow ## Files to Create - src/lib/db/schema/follows.ts - src/lib/social/follows.ts - src/lib/social/feed.ts - src/app/api/users/[id]/follow/route.ts - src/app/(app)/feed/page.tsx - src/components/social/follow-button.tsx ## Success Criteria - Following works correctly - Feed shows relevant activity - Counts accurate - Performance acceptable ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:00.68485604-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:19:00.68485604-05:00"} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8699","title":"Add skeleton loading to SpotlightSearch during semantic rerank","description":"# Task: Add Skeleton Loading to SpotlightSearch ## Context When semantic reranking is enabled, SpotlightSearch shows BM25 results immediately then reranks them. Currently, the only feedback is "Improving..." text in the footer. Users may not notice results are being reordered. ## Current Behavior 1. User types query 2. BM25 results appear immediately 3. Footer shows "Improving..." with sparkle icon 4. Results silently reorder when reranking completes ## Proposed Behavior 1. User types query 2. Skeleton placeholders appear with shimmer effect 3. BM25 results fade in (partial loading state) 4. "Improving..." indicator visible 5. Results smoothly reorder with animation 6. "Semantic" badge replaces "Improving..." ## Implementation ### 1. Skeleton Component for Search Results ```tsx function SearchResultSkeleton() { return (
); } ``` ### 2. Show Skeletons During Initial Search ```tsx // In the results rendering section: {isSearching && results.length === 0 && (
{Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, i) => ( ))}
)} ``` ### 3. Add Reorder Animation ```tsx // Wrap results in AnimatePresence with layout animation {results.map((result, index) => ( {/* Result button content */} ))} ``` ### 4. Shimmer Effect (Optional Enhancement) ```css @keyframes shimmer { 0% { background-position: -200% 0; } 100% { background-position: 200% 0; } } .skeleton-shimmer { background: linear-gradient( 90deg, oklch(0.95 0 0) 0%, oklch(0.90 0 0) 50%, oklch(0.95 0 0) 100% ); background-size: 200% 100%; animation: shimmer 1.5s infinite; } ``` ## Design Rationale - Skeletons communicate "loading" better than spinners - Layout animations make reordering feel smooth, not jarring - Staggered entrance (delay: index * 0.02) creates professional feel - Shimmer effect adds polish (optional) ## Testing 1. Enable semantic mode 2. Type query - see skeletons while loading 3. Results appear with staggered animation 4. When reranking completes, results smoothly reorder 5. Disable semantic mode - results still stagger in 6. Test with prefers-reduced-motion: animations disabled ## Files Modified - apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx ## Dependencies - Requires Framer Motion (already included) - Should match PromptGrid skeleton pattern - Respects reduced motion preference","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:40:58.534780914-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:33:22.563166522-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8699","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-usn5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:43:30.625160247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8699","title":"Add skeleton loading to SpotlightSearch during semantic rerank","description":"# Task: Add Skeleton Loading to SpotlightSearch ## Context When semantic reranking is enabled, SpotlightSearch shows BM25 results immediately then reranks them. Currently, the only feedback is "Improving..." text in the footer. Users may not notice results are being reordered. ## Current Behavior 1. User types query 2. BM25 results appear immediately 3. Footer shows "Improving..." with sparkle icon 4. Results silently reorder when reranking completes ## Proposed Behavior 1. User types query 2. Skeleton placeholders appear with shimmer effect 3. BM25 results fade in (partial loading state) 4. "Improving..." indicator visible 5. Results smoothly reorder with animation 6. "Semantic" badge replaces "Improving..." ## Implementation ### 1. Shimmer Effect (Optional Enhancement) ```css @keyframes shimmer { 0% { background-position: -200% 0; } 100% { background-position: 200% 0; } } .skeleton-shimmer { background: linear-gradient( 90deg, oklch(0.95 0 0) 0%, oklch(0.90 0 0) 50%, oklch(0.95 0 0) 100% ); background-size: 200% 100%; animation: shimmer 1.5s infinite; } ``` ## Design Rationale - Skeletons communicate "loading" better than spinners - Layout animations make reordering feel smooth, not jarring - Staggered entrance (delay: index * 0.02) creates professional feel - Shimmer effect adds polish (optional) ## Testing 1. Test with prefers-reduced-motion: animations disabled ## Files Modified - apps/web/src/components/SpotlightSearch.tsx ## Dependencies - Requires Framer Motion (already included) - Should match PromptGrid skeleton pattern - Respects reduced motion preference","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:40:58.534780914-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:35:25.62844174-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:35:25.62844174-05:00","close_reason":"Added SearchResultSkeleton component and Framer Motion layout animations for smooth result reordering during semantic reranking","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8699","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-usn5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:43:30.625160247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-897","title":"Add error boundaries with fallback UI -- Details: Wrap search, prompt grid, detail modals with error boundaries. Prevent full page crash from component failures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:26:59.23087891-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:53:50.466750981-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:53:50.466750981-05:00","close_reason":"ErrorBoundary component created with fallback UI, retry button, and multiple variants. Providers wrapped with error boundaries for main content and SpotlightSearch."} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8ado","title":"E2E Tests: Ratings & Reviews","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-jhw. ## Task Create comprehensive E2E tests for ratings and reviews system. ## Test Scenarios ### Rating Prompts 1. Rate prompt with stars (1-5) 2. Update existing rating 3. Remove rating 4. Rating reflects in average ### Writing Reviews 5. Write text review 6. Edit review 7. Delete review 8. Review character limits ### Viewing Reviews 9. Reviews display on prompt detail 10. Sort reviews (newest, helpful, rating) 11. Helpful/not helpful voting 12. Review author profile link ### Premium Reviews 13. Premium users can write longer reviews 14. Verified purchase badge (if applicable) ## Files to Create - e2e/ratings/rate.spec.ts - e2e/ratings/reviews.spec.ts - e2e/ratings/display.spec.ts - e2e/lib/ratings-helpers.ts ## Success Criteria - Rating system works end-to-end - Reviews CRUD complete - Voting works correctly ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:22:12.981468428-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:22:12.981468428-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8ado","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-qbem","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:34.252137526-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8ado","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-5x23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:34.382136645-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8cla","title":"Task: E2E test - Prompt detail page navigation and content","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:47:10.080884805-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:08:28.2163965-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:08:28.2163965-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive prompt detail page E2E test suite with 18 tests covering content display, actions (copy/install/download), context input, navigation, mobile bottom sheet, and featured prompts","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-8cla","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:57:10.071614496-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
